channel.title: Weight Watchers on Bell Favorite Recipes
channel.description: Recent content in Weight Watchers on Bell Favorite Recipes

item.title: Spicy Black Bean Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 Medium onion, finely chopped
  • 4 Garlic Cloves, minced
  • 1/2 Tsp Red Pepper Flakes. (or more if you like it with a bit more zing)
  • 45 oz of canned Black Beans (3 15 oz cans)
  • 14 1/2 oz of chicken broth (or vegetable broth)
  • 1 10 oz can of tomatoes with green chilies (such as Rotel Tomatoes)
  • 1 11 oz can of corn

Instructions

  1. Coat bottom of large stockpot with cooking spray. Add onion and garlic and cook, stirring frequently, until onions are soft but not brown, about 5 minutes.
  2. Place one can of beans in blender; add sautéed onion mixture, and red pepper flakes. Cover and blend on high until smooth, about 30 seconds. Pour mixture back into stockpot.
  3. Place second can of beans and broth in blender and puree until smooth; add to stockpot.
  4. Stir third can of beans (do not puree first), chicken stock, tomatoes and green chilies, and corn into stockpot. Bring to a boil, lower heat to medium and simmer for 20 to 25 minutes. Garnish with sour cream.
  5. Yields about 1 1/2 cups of soup per serving.

Billy’s Notes:I couldn’t find 15 oz cans of black beans, so I just dived all my black beans into thirds, for how the recipe plays out.

item.title: Mexican Black Bean and Corn Salad
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1 can of black beans, drained and rinsed
  • 1 can of corn, drained
  • 1/2 cup of salsa (no sugar added)
  • 2 Tbsp non-fat Greek yogurt (or sour cream)
  • salt and pepper to taste

Optional ingredients I’ve tried:

  • Cilantro (chopped up) - added a good lemony and more of a sunny vacation-y flavour to it
  • Chopped up jalapenos - little more zing is never a bad idea!

Pour the beans + corn into a bowl.

item.title: Melt In Your Mouth Chicken Breasts
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 boneless chicken breasts
  • 1 cup Greek yogurt (or mayo if you’re going a little less healthy)
  • 1/2 cup parmesan cheese
  • 1/2 tsp seasoned salt
  • 1/2 tsp black pepper
  • 1 tsp garlic powder

Instructions

  1. Mix mayonnaise or yogurt, cheese and seasonings.
  2. Spread mixture over chicken breasts and place in baking dish.
  3. Bake at 375°F for 30 minutes.

Credit for this recipe goes to Food.com:

Melt-In-Your-Mouth Chicken Breasts Recipe - Food.comThe mayonnaise make these chicken breast very moist and tender–enjoy!

item.title: Low Fat Sweet and Sour Rice
item.description:
Ingredients

Note:  Make the sauce below for 1 cup of pre-cooked rice.  Just double or triple it for however many cups of rice you have.

Make the rice however you make your rice.  This isn’t a rice recipe - it’s more of a sauce recipe.

  • 2 Tbsp low sodium soya sauce
  • 2 Tbsp rice vinegar
  • 1.5 Tbsp pineapple juice  (NOTE: don’t be a dope like me and buy pineapple juice.  Just use the juice from the can of pineapple, noted below)
  • 1 Tbsp tomato paste
  • 1 Tbsp brown sugar
  • 1 Tsp corn starch
  • 1 Tbsp water (for corn starch slurry)
  • 1 Medium carrot, julienned
  • 1 Medium red bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1 Medium green bell pepper, cut thinly
  • 1/2 cup of pineapple chunks (optional, but really, there’s pineapple juice in it for cryin’ out loud.  If you don’t like pineapple, don’t make this)

Instructions

  1. Cook your rice ahead of time however you cook rice.
  2. In a small bowl, mix together the soy sauce, rice vinegar, pineapple juice, tomato paste, and brown sugar to create the sweet and sour sauce. Stir until well combined.
  3. In a separate small bowl, dissolve the cornstarch in 1 tablespoon of water to create a slurry. Set aside.
  4. Heat a large nonstick skillet or wok over medium heat. Add the julienned carrots and sliced bell peppers. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they are slightly softened but still have some crunch.
  5. Pour the sweet and sour sauce into the skillet with the vegetables. Stir well to coat the vegetables evenly.
  6. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Gradually add the cornstarch slurry while stirring to thicken the sauce. Allow it to cook for 2-3 minutes until the sauce reaches the desired consistency.
  7. If using, gently fold in the pineapple chunks and cook for an additional minute to warm them through.
  8. Fluff the cooked rice with a fork and serve it onto plates or bowls. Spoon the sweet and sour vegetable mixture over the rice.

Credit for this recipe goes to Snap Calorie:

item.title: Greek Yogurt Smoothie
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 1/2 cup unsweetened Greek yogurt
  • 3/4 cup unsweetened almond milk
  • 1 tsp stevia (optional)
  • 1 cup of fresh fruit (or drained canned fruit or frozen fruit).  Feel free to mix it up.
  • 1 tbsp chia seeds
  • 2 tbsp oatmeal

Instructions

Chuck it all into a blender.  Drink it.

Weight Watchers Points: 2

Billy’s Notes: The points are pretty much the chia seeds and oatmeal.  If you don’t want the benefits those 2 things bring with them, this is a 0 point smoothie.

item.title: Fruit Protein Pancake
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 3 bananas
  • 2 eggs
  • 3 Tbps rolled oats
  • Olive oil

Instructions

  1. Whisk both eggs in a bowl.
  2. Mash up just 1 of the 3 bananas on a plate, using a fork.  Add to eggs and mix.
  3. Add the rolled oats to the egg/banana mix as well.
  4. Cut up the other 2 bananas in slices.
  5. In a pan (that you can cover), drizzle some olive oil to make sure the bottom is barely covered.
  6. Lay down all of the banana slices evenly around the pan, then pour the egg mixture over them.  Even it out with a spoon or your spatula.
  7. Cover and cook on low for 10 mins.
  8. Flip it over and cook on low for another 5 mins (still covered).  Pro-tip: Saw a neat trick to hold a plate upside down over your pan, tightly, then flip the pan and plate right over - to flip your pancake.  Worked like a charm!
  9. Cut it up and put some syrup or whipped cream or whatever you want to jazz it up!

Weight Watchers Points: 1 point for 1/3 of the pancake.

item.title: Egg Drop Soup
item.description:
Ingredients

  • 4 cups chicken broth
  • 3 eggs
  • 1/3 cup cold water
  • 3 tbsp corn starch

Instructions

  1. Mix the corn starch slurry (the corn starch and the water) in a bowl
  2. Bring the chicken broth to a simmer in a medium pot
  3. Slowly stir the corn starch slurry into the chicken broth
  4. Let it simmer for around 2-3 mins, stirring occasionally
  5. Whisk the eggs in a separate bowl
  6. Slowly stir the eggs into the soup, while stirring.  The more you stir, the finer the egg “strands” will be.  Think the same technique as making scrambled eggs.  Lots of stirring, little chunks.  Less stirring, big chunks.
  7. Let that simmer for a few mins and enjoy.

Billy’s Note:I put a couple of cups of kimchi in this and it gave it some nice color, texture, and spice.  I’m going to put chicken in it next time to get more protein in it.
